title: Boat Rental in Goa: 2026 Guide to Costs and Cruises
html:
Goa's coastline from the Mandovi River to Palolem offers dozens of boat experiences, with rentals starting at INR 3,500 for a two-hour trip. BoatHire24 connects you directly with verified operators, so you can compare options and hire a boat that suits your group.

Where to set off in Goa

Most boat trips depart from Panaji's Captain of Ports jetty, the Ribeirão do Sinquerim beach near Fort Aguada, or the fishing harbour at Malim. South Goa departures centre on Betul beach and Mobor Bay, while the Chapora River mouth serves the Baga-Candolim stretch. Palolem's natural bay is calm for kayaks and small motorboats. You can hire a boat in a few minutes.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Goa?

For self-drive hire, a valid Indian driving licence is required for vessels over 10 hp; the operator confirms exact requirements at booking. Crewed charters include a skipper, so no licence is needed. Always check the specific terms on your booking confirmation. One option worth a look is the Olympic 490 for hire.

For a peaceful morning, launch from Chorao Island and motor up the Mandovi River before 10am; the water is glassy and you will spot kingfishers along the mangroves.

Which boat to rent

  1. Small motorboat (4, 6 persons) Ideal for exploring river islands and nearby beaches. From INR 4,500 for two hours.
  2. Skipper-led speedboat (6, 8 persons) Fast runs to Grande Island for snorkelling. From INR 8,000 for two hours.
  3. Catamaran (10, 12 persons) Stable platform for sunset cruises off Sinquerim. From INR 18,000 for three hours.
  4. Crewed yacht (8 persons) Sailing off the Betul coast; includes refreshments. From INR 25,000 for a half-day.

For a compact vessel that is easy to handle, consider the Olympic 490 for hire through BoatHire24; it is well suited to small groups and shallow coastal waters.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Goa?

Prices for 2026 range from INR 3,500 for a shared tour to INR 45,000 for a fully crewed luxury yacht for a full day. If you own a boat, you can rent out your boat and earn from spare days.

  • Shared sunset cruise on a catamaran: INR 1,500, 2,500 per person.
  • Self-drive motorboat half-day (4 persons): approximately INR 7,000.
  • Catamaran full-day (12 guests, crewed): around INR 28,000.
  • Crewed Azimut or Lagoon yacht for eight hours: INR 40,000, 55,000.

To understand the booking process, read how boat rental works on BoatHire24; it covers payment, cancellation and safety checks. To see how booking works, visit how boat rental works.

Season and weather

The high season runs from November to March, when the northeast monsoon brings calm seas and clear skies. The south-west monsoon (June to September) grounds most small boats. A local wind known as the "Mormugão" breeze picks up in the afternoon, so morning trips are often smoother.

At a glance

  • Price band: INR 3,500, 55,000 per trip
  • Peak months: November to March
  • Typical trip length: 2, 8 hours
  • Motorboat capacity: 4, 12 persons
  • Currency: INR

Questions about boat rental in Goa

Can I rent a boat without a skipper in Goa?

Yes, for self-drive you need a valid Indian driving licence and the operator may require a brief safety briefing.

What is the best time of day for a boat trip in Goa?

Early morning (7am, 10am) offers the flattest water, while sunset cruises are popular for the coastal views.

Are there restrictions on where I can take a boat in Goa?

You must stay within designated coastal zones; the operator will provide a route map and safety notes.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Goa or list your own. Whether you are planning a day trip on the Mandovi or a week-long yacht charter, the platform lets you compare options and book directly. If you own a vessel, you can also rent out your boat to travellers visiting Goa in 2026.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Goa and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
